mnemonics.title: Remembering Replacement Policies
mnemonics.content: LRU: Think 'Least Recently Used' - like Netflix keeps shows you watched recently. FIFO: Like a queue at ticket counter - first in, first out. LFU: Like a popularity contest - least popular gets replaced.

mnemonics.title: Cache Hit vs Miss
mnemonics.content: HIT = Found in cache (Happy - fast access). MISS = Not found (Must go to main memory - slow). Remember: MISS = Must Involve Slower Search

acronyms.usage: Replacement policy that evicts least recently accessed line
Replacement policy that evicts oldest line in set
Replacement policy that evicts least accessed line
Processor that requests data from cache
Formula: Hit time + (Miss rate × Miss penalty)

acronyms.expansion: Least Recently Used - Remember 'Recently Used = Keep it', 'Least Recently = Throw it'

visualTips.visualization: Draw a table with rows as sets and columns as ways (lines per set). Show how memory address maps to set index and then any way can be used. Color-code different memory blocks mapping to same set.
For 4-way set, draw 4 counters. On access to way 0, set all counters higher than 0's counter to increment, set counter 0 to 0. Replace the way with highest counter value.
Think of cache lines as people in a queue. New arrivals go to back, oldest person at front leaves when space is needed.

analogies.analogy: Cache is like a restaurant with limited tables. LRU keeps tables for customers who visited most recently (VIPs get priority). FIFO is like a waiting queue - first customer gets table first.

analogies.concept: Cache Miss Penalty as Shopping Trip
analogies.analogy: Finding item in cache is like having snacks at home (quick). Cache miss is like having to go to supermarket - takes much longer (penalty).
Cache lines are like a small reading list on your desk. If you need a new book and desk is full, you decide which to remove - LRU keeps what you read recently, FIFO removes oldest arrival.
